Almost 200 animals rescued from puppy mill

Cars totaling nearly $200k stolen from dealership

México e Inglaterra, ¿el partido más importante en la historia del país? | Enlace Mundial
items.summary: Nuestros expertos analizan la previa del México vs Inglaterra, un partido que promete hacer historia en la Copa Mundial de la FIFA 2026. La pasión se vive al máximo en la ciudad y todo está listo para un duelo que podría marcar un antes y un después.

items.title: ¿Y si sí? México llega al Coloso de Santa Úrsula en busca de hacer historia ante Inglaterra
items.summary: Aquí no es así y México llega al mítico Estadio Azteca con el objetivo de eliminar a la Inglaterra de Harry Kane y Jude Bellingham. Aguirre y compañía van por la hazaña.

items.title: Jesús Gallardo: “Hoy veremos la mejor versión de México”
items.summary: Jesús Gallardo aseguró que México llega con confianza e ilusión al duelo ante Inglaterra. El lateral destacó el gran momento del equipo, el respaldo de la afición y afirmó que este es un día para hacer historia.

items.title: Davide Ancelotti tras la eliminación de Brasil: “Pequeños errores te dejan fuera”
items.summary: Davide Ancelotti habló tras la eliminación de Brasil y aclaró que el cobrador del penal ya estaba definido antes del partido. Además, reconoció la calidad de Erling Haaland y lo difícil que es frenar a un jugador de nivel mundial.

items.title: Marquinhos: “Es inexplicable lo que pasó”
items.summary: Marquinhos lamentó la derrota 2-1 ante Noruega y asumió la responsabilidad por la eliminación de Brasil. El defensor reconoció la falta de eficacia, pidió perdón a la afición y aseguró que ahora comienza un nuevo ciclo.

items.title: Noruega sorprende a Brasil y clasifica a Cuartos de Final del Mundial 2026.
items.summary: Erling Haaland marcó un doblete para liderar el triunfo 2-1 de Noruega sobre Brasil. Neymar anotó de penal en el descuento, pero los europeos sellaron su pase a los cuartos de final.

items.title: Últimos momentos de Neymar Jr. en Mundiales desesperado y discutiendo
items.summary: El recorrido mundialista de Neymar Jr. llegó a su fin con minutos tensos dentro de la cancha. El brasileño se despidió tras propinar un fuerte patadón a Ødegaard. Tras el cobro del tiro libre, ambos protagonizaron un tenso intercambio de palabras.

items.title: Neymar rompe en llanto tras ser eliminado por Haaland y Noruega
items.summary: Tremendas imágenes del ex futbolista del Barcelona totalmente devastado sobre el césped después de decir adiós a los Mundiales.

items.title: ¡Neymar descuenta de penal! Brasil recortó la distancia ante Noruega
items.summary: Tras una decisión del árbitro en los minutos finales, Brasil recibió un penal a su favor. Neymar Jr. fue el encargado de cobrarlo y convirtió el 2-1, aunque el descuento no alcanzó para evitar la eliminación brasileña de la Copa Mundial de la FIFA 2026.

items.title: Así explican el perdón a Folarin Balogun
items.summary: El Comité Disciplinario de la FIFA dejó en suspenso la sanción del goleador estadounidense por un periodo de prueba de un año, por lo que estará disponible para el juego de Octavos de Final que disputarán contra Bélgica.

items.title: Fierce winds hit US Pacific territories as Super Typhoon Bavi nears Guam
items.summary: U.S. territories in the western Pacific prepare for storm's landfall Monday.

items.title: ¡Doblete de Haaland! Noruega sentencia a Brasil y aseguran el pase a cuartos
items.summary: Erling Haaland volvió a aparecer para liquidar el partido. El delantero noruego sacó un potente disparo raso desde fuera del área para marcar el 2-0 sobre Brasil y sellar el pase de Noruega a los cuartos de final de la Copa Mundial de la FIFA 2026.

items.title: Here’s the biggest news you missed this weekend
items.summary: President Donald Trump on Saturday night delivered an address for America’s 250th birthday on a day roiled by severe weather — from triple-digit temperatures to pouring rain.

items.title: Delta pilot reports a close encounter with a July Fourth firework before landing in Chicago
items.summary: A Delta Air Lines flight and a high-flying firework reportedly came into contact in the sky above Chicago as the city celebrated the Fourth of July holiday

items.title: Democrat Mallory McMorrow suspends her Michigan Senate campaign and scrambles the pivotal race
items.summary: Michigan Democrat Mallory McMorrow has suspended her campaign for the U.S. Senate

items.title: FIFA lifts US player Balogun’s red card suspension at World Cup after Trump calls Infantino
items.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump intervened on behalf of American star forward Folarin Balogun, whose suspension was lifted in a decision that allows him to play in a World Cup match against Belgium on Monday

items.title: National Guard members on patrol in Memphis fatally shoot man during pursuit, police say
items.summary: Tennessee National Guard members assigned by the Trump administration to a crime-fighting patrol in Memphis fatally shot a man who authorities say turned toward the soldiers with a gun during a downtown pursuit

items.title: Interior secretary says administration will use the same company to repair Reflecting Pool
items.summary: The administration has faced scrutiny for awarding a multimillion-dollar, no-bid contract to a company that previously worked on Trump properties.

items.title: 3 firefighters killed in Colorado remembered for their bravery as wildfires churn in the West
items.summary: Wildland firefighters have gathered to pay tribute to three of their own who died after they were trapped by flames a week ago in western Colorado

items.title: At least five killed in shootings and explosions that leave dozens wounded across the U.S.
items.summary: Fourth of July celebrations across the country were marred by violence that killed at least five people and injured scores in multiple cities.

items.title: Y si sí: the phrase uniting Mexico during the World Cup
items.summary: Mexico's World Cup run has inspired a phrase heard across the country: "Y si sí." Or in English: "What if we can?" Anamaria Sayre reports from Mexico City.

items.title: Thunderstorms, heat and wind will hamper efforts to contain Colorado wildfires
items.summary: Thunderstorms with high winds could make it more difficult to contain a massive wildfire that has scorched parts of southern Colorado.

items.title: Trump to meet with Ukraine's Zelenskyy and Syria's al-Sharaa during the NATO summit
items.summary: President Donald Trump plans to meet with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and Syrian President Ahmad al-Sharaa while attending the upcoming NATO summit in Turkey

items.title: National Guard troops fatally shoot a man in downtown Memphis
items.summary: Memphis police say National Guard troops fired their weapons in the early morning hours of July 5, killing a man who was armed with a handgun. State investigators will handle the case.

items.title: Folarin Balogun's ban is lifted by FIFA, allowing U.S. striker to play Belgium
items.summary: FIFA's Disciplinary Committee said it would suspend Balogun's one-game red card ban for a probationary period of one year. The Royal Belgian Football Federation said it was "astonished."

items.title: Firework strikes Delta flight landing at Chicago’s Midway Airport
items.summary: The aircraft landed safely and taxied to the gate, the airline said, adding that the incident is under review.

items.title: Guam and surrounding Pacific islands brace for impact of Super Typhoon Bavi
items.summary: People in the Northern Mariana Islands – remote U.S. territories in the Pacific Ocean – are preparing for Super Typhoon Bavi, which experts say could bring winds of over 180 miles per hour.

items.title: Trump called FIFA before it reversed U.S. star Folarin Balogun’s suspension, source says
items.summary: Trump called FIFA days before it reversed the striker’s ban, a source said, while Belgium says is “investigating all potential options” over the decision.

items.title: 25 dead in ongoing heat wave as storms bring flood and wind threats to the East Coast
items.summary: At least 18 record highs were set on July Fourth as celebrations kicked off in mid-Atlantic cities, including Washington and Baltimore.

items.title: Construction is a man's game. These women are demolishing the barriers
items.summary: Women make up a miniscule 3% of construction workers in Kenya. But a nonprofit group is offering them a way to get into the game ... and to get ahead.

items.title: What We Know About Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s Wedding
items.summary: NBC News is learning new details about Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s wedding at New York City’s Madison Square Garden from two guests who were there. The bride and groom both wore white and the entertainment was provided by Stevie Nicks and Paul McCartney. NBC’s Emilie Ikeda reports in this week’s Sunday Focus.

items.title: Millions Pour Into Tehran to Mourn Late Supreme Leader
items.summary: Mourners are filling the streets of Tehran as part of a days-long memorial for Iran’s former Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei who was killed in the first strikes of the war. NBC’s Richard Engel reports for Sunday TODAY.

items.title: A new law in California will clarify 'sell by' dates to reduce food waste
items.summary: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe speaks with Nick Lapis of Californians Against Waste about the new law that standardizes food label "use by" dates and how that reduces consumer confusion and tons of food waste.

items.title: Pennsylvania's proposed data centers are bringing strangers together in protest
items.summary: Proposals to build six data centers in the small community of Archbald, Pennsylvania, have brought strangers together to fight the plans there — and elsewhere in the state.

items.title: Paul Pelosi in hit-and-run in California, car left with major damage, authorities say
items.summary: The husband of former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi was involved in a hit-and-run in California that left a parked car with "major" damage, authorities said Saturday.

items.title: Pastor freed from prison in China weeks after Trump requested his release
items.summary: A pastor of a prominent underground church who was detained in China in October has been released, less than two months after U.S. President Donald Trump brought up his case when meeting Chinese leader Xi Jinping.

items.title: Trump uses America’s 250 speech to honor military heroes and also push political agendas
items.summary: President Trump concluded the semiquincentennial celebration by reflecting on the nation's history, honoring veterans and pushing his political agenda during his address at the Salute to America event. NBC News’ Ryan Nobles reports on key points from the president's speech.

items.title: Fire breaks out on Brooklyn Bridge during New York's fireworks show
items.summary: A fire broke out on the Brooklyn Bridge in New York during the city’s July Fourth fireworks show

items.title: A timeline of Charlie Kirk's assassination and the arrest of Tyler Robinson
items.summary: The 23-year-old man accused of killing Charlie Kirk will be in court this week for the case's most substantial hearing so far

items.title: Prosecutors to lay out their case against the man accused of killing Charlie Kirk
items.summary: Prosecutors will present their case this week against the man accused of killing conservative activist Charlie Kirk

items.title: Trump touts SAVE America Act during Salute to America 250 speech
items.summary: President Trump highlighted the potential approval of the SAVE America Act during his Salute to America 250 speech, stating his goal to require photo identification to vote and limit the use of mail-in ballots.

items.title: 150 people from 50 countries become US citizens at Mount Vernon on America's 250th birthday
items.summary: A group of 150 people from 50 countries has become U.S. citizens at George Washington's Mount Vernon estate

items.title: Prosecutors argue the man accused of killing Charlie Kirk should stand trial
items.summary: Prosecutors in Charlie Kirk's murder case will aim to convince a judge they have enough evidence to try his accused killer and seek the death penalty

items.title: Female American rower completes record-breaking solo journey
items.summary: A Grand Canyon river rafting guide who aimed to become the first American woman to row across the mid-Pacific solo has completed a record-breaking journey from California to Hawaii

items.title: The best Fourth of July sales to shop right now
items.summary: The best 4th of July sales are happening now. Our editors found worthwhile deals on Apple, Yeti, Medicube and more.

items.title: Chess federation suspends former world champion who accused Naroditsky of cheating
items.summary: The international governing body for chess has suspended former world champion Vladimir Kramnik for at least a year

items.title: New Year's Eve ball drops in Times Square for America's 250th
items.summary: The New Year's Eve ball in Times Square has dropped for America's 250th anniversary, making this the first time the ball has dropped outside of a New Year's celebration.

items.title: 3 dead and others rescued after boat capsizes on Wisconsin lake amid storms
items.summary: Three people died and seven others were rescued after a boat capsized on a Wisconsin lake during a strong storm

items.title: The co-owner of lumber mill has died of injuries from the mill fire and explosion
items.summary: The co-owner of a Maine lumber mill has died from injuries he sustained during a fire and explosion at the facility, bringing to three the number of fatalities

items.title: Erdogan's warm ties with Trump offer Turkey an edge ahead of NATO summit
items.summary: President Donald Trump has berated and belittled many of the European leaders who are expected to attend the upcoming NATO summit in Turkey

items.title: Louisiana's top court halts the criminal case against the state attorney general
items.summary: Louisiana's Supreme Court has halted for now the criminal case against state Attorney General Liz Murrill

items.title: Tribes hope Farm Bill can feed more people and preserve Indigenous culture
items.summary: Dawn and Cassius Spears would like to expand their farm’s reach beyond their farm stand, but it’s challenging

items.title: Latin Mass, but faithful to the pope: A Minnesota church shows a different way during schism crisis
items.summary: Priests at the Church of Saint Agnes in St. Paul, Minnesota, celebrate a traditional Latin Rite Mass every Sunday, even though the practice has become a symbol of cultural and political battles among some Catholics

items.title: Man with a Tibetan flag dies after setting himself on fire in front of UN HQ
items.summary: Officials say a man holding a Tibetan flag in front of the United Nations headquarters in New York set himself on fire and died

items.title: Ransom notes in Nancy Guthrie case still being investigated as legitimate, FBI says
items.summary: The FBI has discounted some of the ransom notes that surfaced after the disappearance of Nancy Guthrie as extortion attempts

items.title: Head of the Chicago Police Department to retire after 3 years in the position
items.summary: The head of the Chicago Police Department has announced that he will retire after a short tenure leading the nation’s second-largest police force

items.title: John Dickinson’s missing 1776 signature haunts his legacy
items.summary: Jane Calvert has spent 25 years championing John Dickinson, a lesser-known Revolutionary War figure

items.title: GOP prepared to block vote to replace Feinstein on Senate Judiciary
items.summary: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer said on Tuesday that he hopes to replace Democratic Sen. Dianne Feinstein on the Senate Judiciary Committee with Sen. Ben Cardin of Maryland and aims to set up a floor vote on the issue this afternoon, which Republicans are expected to block.

items.title: Supreme Court seems sympathetic to postal worker who didn't work Sundays in dispute over religious accommodations
items.summary: The Supreme Court seemed to side with a former mail carrier, an evangelical Christian, who says the US Postal Service failed to accommodate his request to not work on Sundays.

items.title: Anti-abortion doctors urge Supreme Court to keep mifepristone restrictions in place
items.summary: A group of doctors opposed to abortion asked the Supreme Court Tuesday to restrict access to a key medication abortion drug while other legal challenges play out, as Wednesday night's deadline for the court to act approaches.

items.title: McCarthy makes plea for Republicans to back debt ceiling plan
items.summary: Speaker Kevin McCarthy made a plea to House Republicans during a closed-door meeting Tuesday morning to back his debt ceiling plan, telling them that although it doesn't have to include everything they want, it will help get him to the negotiating table with President Joe Biden.

items.title: Fulton County, Georgia, jail leadership resigns after inmate's death and accusations of unsanitary conditions
items.summary: Three officials at the Fulton County, Georgia, jail have stepped down amid an investigation into the death of an inmate whose family said was housed in a filthy, bug-infested cell that "was not fit for a diseased animal."

items.title: China's economy is off to a solid start, rising 4.5% in Q1 2023
items.summary: China's economy is off to a solid start in 2023 following its emergence from three years of strict pandemic restrictions.

items.title: He was free for 2 years. Now Crosley Green is back in prison for a crime he says he didn't commit
items.summary: A Florida man who served three decades behind bars for a murder he says he didn't commit returned to prison Monday after spending the past two years building a life outside prison walls.

items.title: Family of victim and survivors of Indianapolis FedEx mass shooting file lawsuit against gun magazine manufacturer and distributors
items.summary: The family of a victim and several survivors of a mass shooting at a FedEx facility in Indianapolis filed a lawsuit against companies involved in the manufacturing, marketing and sale of the high capacity magazine used by the gunman who killed 8 people and injured several others in 2021.

items.title: McDonald's is upgrading its burgers
items.summary: McDonald's, which has been focusing on upgrading its core items to boost sales, is rolling out a series of changes designed to improve its signature burgers.

items.title: Google-parent stock drops on fears it could lose search market share to AI-powered rivals
items.summary: Shares of Google-parent Alphabet fell more than 3% in early trading Monday after a report sparked concerns that its core search engine could lose market share to AI-powered rivals, including Microsoft's Bing.

items.title: Tennessee Air National Guardsman applied to be a hitman online, the FBI says. It was a spoof website and now he's facing charges
items.summary: A Tennessee Air National Guardsman is facing charges after applying to be a hitman on a spoof "rent-a-hitman" website, according to the Department of Justice.

items.title: Even when wives make as much as husbands, they still do more at home
items.summary: • Four out of the five US metro areas with the lowest unemployment are in Florida. Here's why • Opinion: The overlooked problem with raising the retirement age for Social Security
